senses.definition: A non-standard or foreign spelling of apartments, meaning separate places to live in a building.
examples: They rented two appartements near the city center.
Most of the appartements in this block are very small.

senses.definition: Played or expressed with very strong emotion and passion, especially in music.
examples: The pianist gave an appassionata performance of the sonata.
Her singing became more appassionata in the final act.

senses.definition: In a very passionate and emotional way, especially in musical directions.
examples: The piece should be played appassionatamente here.
He spoke appassionatamente about his homeland.

senses.definition: Very passionate or full of strong emotion, especially in an artistic performance; mainly used in musical or literary contexts.
examples: Her appassionate delivery moved the audience to tears.
The novel is written in an appassionate, lyrical style.

senses.definition: As a musical direction, meaning to play in a very passionate and emotional way.
examples: The score is marked appassionato in the final movement.
She repeated the theme appassionato and louder.

senses.definition: Showing or full of strong passion or emotion, especially in performance.
examples: His appassionato style makes every concert exciting.
The concerto has a slow, appassionato middle section.

senses.definition: An archaic form meaning to satisfy or fill, especially hunger; now not used in modern English.
examples: They hoped to appast their hunger with simple bread and cheese.
A small meal was enough to appast the tired travellers.

senses.definition: An obsolete or dialect word meaning stunned, dazed, or struck on the hand.
examples: He stood appaume after the sudden blow.
The appaume soldier could not lift his shield.

senses.definition: In heraldry, describing a hand shown open with the palm facing the viewer.
examples: The shield showed a red hand appaumee on a white field.
An appaumee hand was added to the family coat of arms.

senses.definition: An archaic verb meaning to satisfy, calm, or appease a person or feeling.
examples: Nothing could appay his deep sorrow.
She tried to appay the angry crowd with kind words.

senses.definition: An archaic form meaning satisfied or pleased, often after a desire has been met.
examples: He rose from the table well appayd with the meal.
Her curiosity was fully appayd by his long story.

senses.definition: Archaic present participle of appay, meaning satisfying or calming.
examples: She was appaying their fears with gentle words.
The king rode among the people, appaying their anger.

senses.definition: Archaic third person singular of appay, meaning satisfies or calms.
examples: No gift appays his endless ambition.
Her smile appays his troubled heart.

senses.definition: A shortened form of 'approved' used in some informal notes and records.
examples: The manager initialed the form as 'appd'.
Once appd, the request can be processed.

senses.definition: An obsolete form meaning to accuse someone of a crime or wrongdoing.
examples: They did appeach him of treason before the king.
Many were unjustly appeached by their enemies.

senses.definition: Past form of the archaic verb appeach, meaning accused of a crime or serious fault.
examples: He was appeached before the council for his actions.
Several nobles were appeached and imprisoned.

senses.definition: An old term for a person who accuses someone of a crime or serious wrong.
examples: The appeacher stood before the court and gave his evidence.
His secret appeacher was later discovered.

senses.definition: Archaic third person singular of appeach, meaning accuses someone.
examples: He appeaches his neighbour of theft.
The letter appeaches the officer of corruption.

senses.definition: Archaic present participle of appeach, meaning accusing someone of wrongdoing.
examples: He was appeaching his rivals before the king.
The witnesses were appeaching several of the merchants.

senses.definition: An obsolete form meaning accusation or the act of accusing, especially of a crime.
examples: The appeachment of the duke shocked the whole court.
He lived under the constant fear of appeachment.

senses.definition: Plural of appeachment, meaning accusations or acts of accusing people of crimes.
examples: Many appeachments were made against the former ministers.
The chronicle lists several appeachments in that year.

senses.definition: To make a serious or urgent request for something, especially help, money, or information.
examples: The charity is appealing for donations after the flood.
Local leaders appealed to the public for calm.

senses.definition: To formally ask a court or person in power to change a decision, especially a legal judgment.
examples: He decided to appeal against the prison sentence.
They plan to appeal the decision in a higher court.

senses.definition: To be attractive or interesting to someone.
examples: The idea of working abroad really appeals to me.
Their new product will appeal to young people.

senses.definition: A serious or urgent request for help, money, or information.
examples: The police made an appeal for witnesses.
They launched a television appeal to raise money.

senses.definition: The quality that makes someone or something attractive, interesting, or enjoyable.
examples: The city has a special appeal for art lovers.
The main appeal of the job is the high salary.

senses.definition: A formal request to a court or person in authority to change a decision.
examples: Her appeal was rejected by the Supreme Court.
He is preparing an appeal against the verdict.

senses.definition: Legal possibilities or conditions under which a decision can be appealed.
examples: Different jurisdictions have different appealabilities for such cases.
The contract limits the appealabilities of arbitration awards.

senses.definition: The quality of a decision or case that allows it to be taken to a higher court for review.
examples: The appealability of the ruling was questioned by lawyers.
Statutes define the appealability of interim orders.

senses.definition: That can be brought to a higher court to be changed or reviewed.
examples: The decision is not appealable under current law.
Only final judgments are appealable in this system.

senses.definition: Past form of appeal: made a serious request for help, money, or information.
examples: The charity appealed for more volunteers last year.
She appealed to her friends for support.

senses.definition: Past form of appeal: formally asked a higher court or authority to change a decision.
examples: He appealed against the sentence but lost.
The company appealed the tax ruling.

senses.definition: Past form of appeal: seemed attractive or interesting to someone.
examples: The job appealed to him because of the travel opportunities.
The offer never really appealed to us.

senses.definition: A person or organization that makes an appeal, especially for help or to a higher court.
examples: The appealer addressed the nation on television.
The court ruled in favor of the appealer.

senses.definition: People or organizations that make appeals, especially to the public or to a higher court.
examples: The charity praised all the appealers who spoke on its behalf.
The court heard arguments from both appealers and respondents.

senses.definition: Attractive, interesting, or pleasant in a way that makes you want something.
examples: The idea of living abroad is very appealing.
They designed the website to be more appealing to young users.

senses.definition: Showing that you want help or sympathy.
examples: The puppy gave her an appealing look.
He smiled in an appealing way, hoping for forgiveness.

senses.definition: In a way that is attractive or interesting.
examples: The dish was appealingly presented on the plate.
Bright colors were appealingly used throughout the brochure.

senses.definition: In a way that shows a desire for help or sympathy.
examples: The child looked appealingly at her mother.
He smiled appealingly, hoping she would say yes.

senses.definition: The quality of being attractive, interesting, or pleasing.
examples: The appealingness of the offer depended on the salary.
Designers focused on the product's overall appealingness.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to different kinds or aspects of being appealing.
examples: They compared the various appealingnesses of the designs.
Different markets value different appealingnesses in a product.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appeal: makes a serious request for something.
examples: The organization regularly appeals for donations.
She appeals to her staff for patience during busy periods.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appeal: formally asks a higher court or authority to change a decision.
examples: He appeals against the ruling every year.
The company appeals the decision whenever possible.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appeal: is attractive or interesting to someone.
examples: The course appeals to students of all ages.
This style of music appeals mainly to teenagers.

senses.definition: Plural of appeal: serious requests for help, money, or information.
examples: Their appeals for help were ignored.
The TV station broadcast several appeals for witnesses.

senses.definition: To start to be seen or to come into sight.
examples: A rainbow appeared after the storm.
His name appeared on the screen.

senses.definition: To seem or give the impression of being a particular way.
examples: She appears very confident in meetings.
It appears that we have made a mistake.

senses.definition: To take part in a performance, film, show, or public event.
examples: He appeared in several movies last year.
She will appear on a talk show tonight.

senses.definition: The way someone or something looks to other people.
examples: She is very careful about her appearance.
The building's modern appearance surprised us.

senses.definition: The act of coming to a place, especially in public or in court.
examples: The singer's first public appearance was at the age of ten.
His appearance in court lasted only ten minutes.

senses.definition: What something seems to be, which may be different from the truth.
examples: Appearances can be deceptive.
They tried to keep up appearances despite their problems.

senses.definition: Having a particular kind of appearance; used after an adverb to describe how something looks.
examples: He was a strange-appearanced man with bright green eyes.
The town was poor-appearanced but friendly.

senses.definition: Plural of appearance: the way people or things look.
examples: Their appearances had changed a lot since school.
The shop focuses on improving product appearances.

senses.definition: What things seem to be rather than what they really are.
examples: By all appearances, the project was a success.
He tried to keep up appearances after losing his job.

senses.definition: Past form of appear: came into sight or became visible.
examples: A small boat appeared on the horizon.
Her name suddenly appeared on my phone.

senses.definition: Past form of appear: seemed or gave the impression of being a certain way.
examples: He appeared nervous during the interview.
It appeared that nobody knew the answer.

senses.definition: Past form of appear: took part in a performance, film, or public event.
examples: She appeared in several popular TV series.
The author appeared at a book signing yesterday.

senses.definition: A person who appears, especially in a legal or formal context.
examples: The appearer signed the document before the notary.
Each appearer confirmed their identity to the court.

senses.definition: People who appear, especially in court or in a formal document.
examples: The judge asked the appearers to stand.
All appearers were listed at the end of the contract.

senses.definition: Present participle of appear: coming into sight or starting to be seen.
examples: Dark clouds are appearing on the horizon.
Her name kept appearing in the news.

senses.definition: Present participle of appear: taking part in a performance, program, or event.
examples: She is appearing in a new play this month.
Several experts are appearing on the panel.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appear: comes into sight or becomes visible.
examples: The moon appears early in winter.
Her name appears at the top of the list.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appear: seems or gives the impression of being a certain way.
examples: It appears that we are late.
She appears very confident in class.

senses.definition: Third person singular of appear: takes part in a performance, show, or public event.
examples: He regularly appears on national television.
The lawyer appears in court almost every day.

senses.definition: That can be calmed, satisfied, or made less angry.
examples: Fortunately, the crowd was still appeasable.
Her anger was hardly appeasable after the insult.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to be calmed or satisfied.
examples: His appeasableness made negotiations much easier.
The appeasableness of the protesters was uncertain.

senses.definition: In a way that can be calmed or satisfied.
examples: The crowd behaved appeasably after the announcement.
He spoke appeasably, trying to reduce the tension.

senses.definition: To make someone less angry or stop them from attacking by giving them what they want or calming them.
examples: The government tried to appease the protesters with new promises.
Nothing she said could appease his anger.

senses.definition: To make an unpleasant feeling, such as fear or guilt, less strong.
examples: They donated money to appease their guilty consciences.
A short walk helped to appease his anxiety.

senses.definition: Past form of appease: made someone less angry or less violent, often by giving them what they want.
examples: The angry customers were partly appeased by a refund.
The apology appeased the committee for a time.

senses.definition: The act of calming someone or something by giving them what they want, often used disapprovingly in politics.
examples: The policy of appeasement failed to prevent war.
Critics saw the agreement as a dangerous appeasement of the dictator.

senses.definition: The act of making an unpleasant feeling less strong.
examples: Eating chocolate brought a brief appeasement of her stress.
Meditation can offer some appeasement of anxiety.

senses.definition: Plural of appeasement: acts of calming people or situations by giving in to demands.
examples: The government was criticized for repeated appeasements of extremists.
These small appeasements did little to solve the real problem.

senses.definition: Someone who tries to keep peace by giving in to other people's demands, often seen negatively.
examples: He was called an appeaser for agreeing to their terms.
History has not been kind to the appeasers of that regime.

senses.definition: People who try to keep peace by giving others what they want, often seen as too weak.
examples: The appeasers in the cabinet supported the compromise.
Critics argued that the appeasers had encouraged further aggression.
